One endpoint is slower than the others from your browser.

Measured Fact: Your connection to app.chronoscope.dev is stable, but api.service.net is experiencing intermittent latency spikes.

Interpretation: Since other endpoints are fast, your local Wi-Fi and core ISP connection are likely fine. The issue is likely specific to the route to the API, or the API itself.

CHRONOSCOPE
START TEST
OVERVIEW
LIVE
INVESTIGATE
REPORT
Investigate Evidence

Detailed endpoint diagnostics and what the browser can see

MEASURED FROM YOUR BROWSER

What we can definitively see from your current environment.

app.chronoscope.dev
STABLE
AVG LATENCY
42 ms
JITTER
5 ms
FAILURES
0%
Full diagnostic visibility (Same-Origin)
api.service.net
SPIKING
AVG LATENCY
185 ms
JITTER
45 ms
FAILURES
0%
Hidden by server (CORS limits)
The browser is not permitted to see exactly where time is spent (DNS, TCP, or server delay). We can only measure total trip time.
NEEDS OUTSIDE VALIDATION

Data required to prove whether the issue is local to you.

Check from Outside Vantage Points

Right now, we only know what your specific browser is experiencing. An outside check will prove if this slowness happens to everyone globally, or just your local connection.

Run global test
Check with Local Agent
NOT INSTALLED

A desktop agent can bypass browser security limits to measure precise TCP/TLS times and network routing directly from your machine.

Why do we separate this?
We measure latency, but we never guess the root cause without proof. Separating facts from interpretation prevents falsely blaming your ISP or a specific server.

CHRONOSCOPE
START TEST
OVERVIEW
LIVE
INVESTIGATE
REPORT
Diagnostic Report

Generated on May 15, 2026

Copy Summary
Share Link
CHRONOSCOPE
Degraded Connection Detected

One endpoint (api.service.net) is significantly slower than the others from this browser.

MEASURED FACTS
DURATION
2m 45s
SAMPLES
1,650
ENDPOINT COMPARISON
ENDPOINT	AVG LATENCY	JITTER	STATUS
app.chronoscope.dev	42 ms	±5 ms	STABLE
api.service.net	185 ms	±45 ms	SPIKING
cdn.assets.io	28 ms	±2 ms	STABLE
BROWSER LIMITATIONS

The spiking endpoint (api.service.net) hides detailed timing data. Because of this, the browser cannot determine whether the delay is caused by DNS resolution, TCP connection, TLS handshake, or server processing time.

RECOMMENDED NEXT STEP

Run an outside validation test to determine if this latency is isolated to your local network/ISP, or if it is affecting all users globally.

ID: ch_req_98b72x9f • View live: chronoscope.dev/r/98b72x9f
RETURN TO DASHBOARD
